Content is entirely a download interface with no scientific content."}, {"source_url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10219392/", "source_title": "The Use of Warm Air for Solvent Evaporation in Adhesive Dentistry", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.65, "overall_relevance": 0.2, "year": 2023, "authors": ["Bourgi R.", "Hardan L."], "venue": "PMC / Dental Materials", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "A systematic review and meta-analysis of the effect of warm air blowing for solvent evaporation on bond strength of resin-based adhesive materials found statistically significant improvements for etch-and-rinse adhesives, self-etch adhesives, and silane-based materials across 27 quantitative studies.", "direct_quote": "A total of 6626 articles were retrieved from all databases. From this, 28 articles were included in the qualitative analysis, and 27 remained for the quantitative analysis. The results of the meta-analysis for etch-and-rinse adhesives revealed that the use of warm air for solvent evaporation was statistically significantly higher (p = 0.005). For self-etch adhesives and silane-based materials, this effect was observed too", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.22, "confidence": 0.85, "perspective": "Methodological", "entities": ["meta-analysis", "warm air", "solvent evaporation", "bond strength", "etch-and-rinse", "self-etch", "silane"]}], "evidence_summary": "Dental adhesive meta-analysis on warm air effects on bond strength.
